Framework · free to use

2026 B2B AI adoption index.

A single composite score (0-100) measuring AI adoption depth at B2B mid-market companies, with the underlying methodology, May 2026 sample distribution, and benchmark scores by industry and company size. Designed for journalists, analysts, and CEOs benchmarking their own organizations.

About the Index

What it measures and citation rules

What the index measures: A single 0-100 composite score capturing how deeply a B2B mid-market company has actually adopted AI in production work — not awareness, not licenses bought, but real workflow integration and measurable outcomes.

Why a single number: Aggregated scores are easier to cite, benchmark against, and track over time. The underlying methodology (below) is the substance; the number is the headline.

Permission to cite: Yes. Attribution: "Treetop Growth Strategy, 2026 B2B AI Adoption Index, May 2026 — treetopgrowthstrategy.com/b2b-ai-adoption-index-2026". Stable URL; refreshed quarterly.

Headline numbers — May 2026

Sample of ~60 B2B mid-market companies

MetricMay 2026 scoreDirection vs Q1 2026
Overall sample median42+5
75th percentile64+7
25th percentile24+3
Top decile78+5
Bottom decile12+2

Reading the score: 0-25 = Exploring; 26-50 = Piloting; 51-75 = Operationalizing; 76-100 = Compounding. Median company is mid-Piloting in May 2026.

The 10 underlying dimensions

How the score is computed

Each company is scored 0-10 on each of 10 dimensions. Total: 0-100.

#DimensionWhat 10/10 looks like
1Leadership commitmentCEO uses AI daily; AI is a documented exec priority
2Named ownershipOne human accountable with 10+ hrs/week protected
3Platform consolidationOne LLM platform deeply adopted across functions
4Production workflows5+ workflows in daily production use across functions
5Documentation disciplineEach workflow has a one-page doc; prompt library maintained
6MeasurementBaseline + post-rollout metrics tracked quarterly
7Team adoption breadth80%+ of affected team uses AI tools weekly
8Policy & governanceWritten policy published; data classes defined; incident process exists
9Hiring alignmentAI fluency is a documented role requirement; assessed in interviews
10Investment compoundingYear-over-year increase in workflows; budget allocated for next year
Distribution by company size — May 2026

Where companies cluster

Company sizeMedian Index scoreRange (25-75)% scoring 50+
10-25 people3822-58~35%
25-50 people4225-60~40%
50-100 people4528-65~45%
100-250 people4830-70~50%

Observation: Company size correlates weakly with Index score in our sample. The strongest predictor is leadership commitment (dimension 1), not size. Some 25-person companies score 70+; some 200-person companies score below 30.

Distribution by industry — May 2026

Which verticals lead

IndustryMedian Index score% scoring 50+
B2B SaaS52~55%
Professional services (agencies, consulting)48~50%
E-commerce / DTC45~45%
Financial services38~35%
Manufacturing (mid-market industrial)35~30%
Healthcare (private practices)32~25%
Nonprofit28~20%
Real estate (brokerages)28~20%
Year-over-year movement

Index trajectory since 2024

DateSample medianTop quartileBottom quartile
May 202418386
Nov 202424468
May 2025325614
Nov 2025376020
May 2026426424

Observation: The median has roughly doubled over 24 months. The bottom quartile has moved up faster than the top — the floor is rising. The top quartile continues advancing but at a slower rate; pushing from 60 to 80 is harder than 30 to 50.

How to score your own company

Self-assessment

Score honestly 0-10 on each dimension. Sum for your Index score.

  1. Leadership commitment: 0 = CEO doesn't use AI; 5 = uses occasionally; 10 = daily, publicly, models for team.
  2. Named ownership: 0 = no one owns it; 5 = part-time owner without protected time; 10 = full-time-equivalent owner with mandate.
  3. Platform consolidation: 0 = sprawl of personal tools; 5 = enterprise license but inconsistent use; 10 = one platform, deep adoption.
  4. Production workflows: 0 = none; 5 = 1-2 in production; 10 = 5+ across functions.
  5. Documentation: 0 = nothing documented; 5 = some prompts saved; 10 = workflow docs maintained, prompt library lives.
  6. Measurement: 0 = no baselines; 5 = some workflows measured; 10 = systematic measurement + quarterly review.
  7. Team adoption: 0 = no one uses; 5 = ~40% weekly active; 10 = 80%+ weekly active in affected functions.
  8. Policy: 0 = no policy; 5 = informal rules; 10 = published policy + incident process.
  9. Hiring alignment: 0 = not assessed; 5 = mentioned in some role specs; 10 = required and assessed in interviews.
  10. Investment compounding: 0 = flat or shrinking; 5 = maintained; 10 = systematic year-over-year growth.
Interpretation

What your score means

  • 0-25 (Exploring): AI usage is individual, not organizational. Big upside available.
  • 26-50 (Piloting): Some workflows in production, ownership unclear. Most common state in May 2026.
  • 51-75 (Operationalizing): Real adoption with infrastructure. Continued investment compounds.
  • 76-100 (Compounding): AI fluency is part of how the company operates. Hard to reach; durable advantage.
Methodology

How the Index is computed

  • Sample: ~60 B2B mid-market companies, \$5M-\$50M ARR, Q4 2024-Q2 2026.
  • Each company scored on 10 dimensions during engagement intake, mid-engagement review, or post-engagement follow-up.
  • Scoring done by Treetop consultants based on observation; not self-report.
  • Year-over-year tracking includes companies scored at multiple points in time.
  • Sample skews toward B2B SaaS and professional services; light coverage of pure consumer brands.
  • Refresh cadence: quarterly. Last refresh: May 2026.

Methodology caveat: The sample is biased toward companies investing seriously in AI rollout (they hired a consultancy). Treat the absolute numbers as biased upward; treat year-over-year deltas and distributional patterns as more reliable.
